programs.title: Marcus Wareing's Kitchen Garden Tales
programs.desc: Marcus investigates unusual food flavours that he can use in cooking. From foraging for edible produce to experimenting with his smoker to make chilli butter.

programs.title: My Market Kitchen
programs.desc: Laura teams up with Chef Jack Ingram, cooking Tommy Ruffs with apple, beetroot and cucumber on the banks of the River Torrens. Mike cooks Vietnamese fish skewers in the market kitchen.

programs.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programs.sub_title: Corn: From the Kernel to the Husk
programs.desc: Today is all about corn. Recipes include a refreshing corn zucchini minestra, a polenta cacio e pere, and a mix of vegetables that can be served as a side dish - grilled corn, apricots, and portobellos.

programs.title: Tastes And Traditions
programs.desc: In this episode of Tastes and Traditions, we explore flavorful, easy-to-make dishes that bring comfort and joy to the table.

programs.title: Jamie at Home
programs.desc: Jamie is nearly knocked off his feet in this episode as he braves the gales to collect a variety of salad. Unlikely as it seems, the garden is far from boring at this time of year and offers up a varied collection of crunchy, colourful delights.

programs.title: Food Safari
programs.desc: Author Dennis Valcich runs through the key ingredients used in Croatian cooking and demonstrates a recipe from the Dalmatian coast - artichoke with potatoes and broad beans. Also from Dalmatia is Melbourne chef Ino Kuvacic, who makes fish stew on polenta (brodet), while home cook Branka Roncevic makes some delicious cabbage rolls.
